personas.name: Anxious Student Seeking Fast Relief
personas.description: A student or young adult who uses Wysa to manage anxiety, stress, overthinking, and emotional overwhelm through quick, accessible coping support.
goals: Feel calmer and more in control of thoughts
pain_points: Experiences anxiety spikes and stress overload
Cannot always identify the exact cause of distress
Needs support immediately rather than later
context: Uses the app during stressful days, study pressure, or emotional overwhelm
Often opens the app in short sessions when anxious thoughts increase
constraints: Support must be quick to access
Exercises should be practical and easy to follow during distress

personas.name: User Recovering from Depression and Negative Thought Cycles
personas.description: A user dealing with depression, emotional pain, or recurring negative thoughts who relies on Wysa for reflection, encouragement, and mental health recovery support.
goals: Build a more positive and stable mindset
pain_points: May feel emotionally low, hopeless, or overwhelmed
Needs a supportive tool between harder moments
context: Uses the app during mental health recovery and emotionally difficult periods
May rely on the app when professional support is unavailable or delayed
constraints: Support should feel compassionate and safe
Advice should be sensitive to serious mental health situations

personas.name: User Seeking a Safe Non-Judgmental Companion
personas.description: A user who values Wysa as a private, judgment-free space to talk, vent, reflect, and feel heard when other support is not available.
goals: Have someone or something available to talk to anytime
Express feelings without fear of judgment
Feel emotionally supported and listened to
pain_points: Does not always have a trusted person available
Needs privacy when discussing difficult feelings
May feel isolated or uncomfortable opening up to others
context: Uses the app alone, often during emotionally difficult moments
May turn to the app late at night or when immediate human support is unavailable
constraints: The app should maintain a supportive and non-judgmental tone

personas.name: Budget-Conscious User Evaluating Free vs Paid Value
personas.description: A user whose satisfaction depends heavily on whether useful Wysa features are available without unexpected paywalls, pricing frustration, or unclear subscription value.
goals: Access meaningful mental health support at low cost
Understand which features are free and which are paid
Decide whether premium features are worth the price
pain_points: Feels frustrated when important features are locked behind payment
May perceive the app as too expensive
Wants transparent pricing and feature access
context: Explores the app while comparing free and premium support options
May be trying the app before deciding whether to subscribe
constraints: Pricing and feature access must be clearly explained
Essential support should not feel misleadingly unavailable

personas.name: User Frustrated by Repetition, Weak Memory, or App Quality Issues
personas.description: A user who wants helpful, coherent, and reliable AI support but becomes dissatisfied when conversations feel repetitive, shallow, forgetful, or technically frustrating.
goals: Have conversations that feel coherent over time
Use an app that works reliably without frustration
pain_points: Experiences repetitive or redundant AI responses
Feels the app does not remember prior conversations well
May encounter usability or quality issues that reduce trust
context: Uses the app repeatedly over time and notices conversation quality patterns
Compares the app's usefulness against expectations for intelligent emotional support
constraints: Responses should feel context-aware and non-repetitive
The app should behave consistently and reliably
